downstream_desc: Customer country of residence. Join-enriched via Dim_Customer.CountryID → Dim_Country.Name. (Tier 2 — SP_AML_Documents_Dashboard, join-enriched via Dim_Country)
upstream_desc: Full country name in English. Unique per row. Used in UI dropdowns, compliance documents, and analytical reports. (Tier 1 - Dictionary.Country upstream wiki)

merge.merged_desc: Customer country of residence. Full country name in English, used in compliance documents and analytical reports. Join-enriched via Dim_Customer.CountryID → Dim_Country.Name.
merge.attribution: (Tier 1 - Dim_Country.Name; Tier 2 - SP_AML_Documents_Dashboard for join enrichment via Dim_Country)
merge.notes: Upstream adds business context: English-language country name used in compliance/reporting. Downstream mechanics (join path) preserved.

upstream_desc: Manual override name for the marketing region, from Ext_Dim_Country. May differ from Region (e.g., Albania: Region=ROE, MarketingRegionManualName=CEE). Used when the automated MarketingRegion label needs a business-friendly correction. (Tier 3 - Ext_Dim_Country live data)

merge.merged_desc: Marketing team region classification (e.g., 'Arabic', 'French', 'Norway', 'ROW'). Manual override name from Ext_Dim_Country; may differ from the automated Region label (e.g., Albania: Region=ROE but MarketingRegionManualName=CEE). Used when the automated MarketingRegion needs a business-friendly correction. (Tier 2 - Dim_Country.MarketingRegionManualName; Tier 3 - Ext_Dim_Country live data)
merge.notes: Upstream clarifies this is a manual override that can diverge from automated Region, adds concrete example

downstream_desc: Customer's country name at snapshot date (Tier 1 - DWH_dbo.Dim_Country.Name via Fact_SnapshotCustomer.CountryID)
upstream_desc: Customer's registered country. DEFAULT 0. Source: Ext_FSC_Real_Customer_Customer.CountryID (CC). FK to Dim_Country. Key filter for valid customer segmentation (CountryID=250 excluded). (Tier 2 - SP_Fact_SnapshotCustomer)

merge.merged_desc: Customer's registered country name at snapshot date. DEFAULT 0. Sourced from Ext_FSC_Real_Customer_Customer.CountryID (CC), FK to Dim_Country. CountryID=250 is excluded as invalid for customer segmentation. (Tier 1 - Dim_Country.Name via Fact_SnapshotCustomer.CountryID; Tier 2 - SP_Fact_SnapshotCustomer)
merge.notes: Upstream adds DEFAULT 0, CC source system, and CountryID=250 exclusion rule — all useful for analysts

upstream_desc: Marketing sub-channel. Resolved from Dim_Channel.SubChannel via Dim_Affiliate.SubChannelID. ISNULL(,'Direct'). Values: Direct, Google Brand, Affiliate, etc. (Tier 2 — SP_CIDFirstDates, Dim_Channel)

merge.merged_desc: Marketing sub-channel detail. Resolved from Dim_Channel.SubChannel via Dim_Affiliate.SubChannelID. ISNULL defaults to 'Direct'. Values include Direct, Google Brand, Affiliate, etc. (Tier 2 - SP_CIDFirstDates, Dim_Channel)
merge.notes: Upstream adds resolution path through Dim_Affiliate, ISNULL default, and sample values

downstream_desc: Affiliate serial ID for affiliate-acquired customers; NULL for direct/organic (Tier 2 - BI_DB_CIDFirstDates.SerialID)
upstream_desc: Affiliate (partner) ID under which the customer was acquired. Sourced from Dim_Customer.AffiliateID (renamed). (Tier 1 — Customer.CustomerStatic)

merge.merged_desc: Affiliate (partner) ID under which the customer was acquired; NULL for direct/organic. Sourced from Dim_Customer.AffiliateID (renamed to SerialID in BI_DB_CIDFirstDates). Originates from Customer.CustomerStatic. (Tier 1 - Customer.CustomerStatic; Tier 2 - BI_DB_CIDFirstDates.SerialID)
merge.notes: Upstream provides Tier 1 origin (CustomerStatic) and clarifies the rename from AffiliateID to SerialID

downstream_desc: Loyalty tier at EOD: 'LowBronze', 'HighBronze', 'Silver', 'Gold', 'Platinum', 'Platinum Plus', 'Diamond' (Tier 2 - Dim_PlayerLevel.Name with LowBronze/HighBronze split at $1K equity)
upstream_desc: Tier display name: Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Platinum Plus, Diamond, Internal, N/A. Used in BackOffice reporting JOINs and customer-facing UI. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, Dictionary.PlayerLevel)

merge.merged_desc: Loyalty tier at end-of-day. Base values from Dim_PlayerLevel: Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Platinum Plus, Diamond, Internal, N/A. Local logic splits Bronze into LowBronze (< $1K equity) and HighBronze (>= $1K equity). Used in BackOffice reporting and customer-facing UI. (Tier 1 - Dictionary.PlayerLevel; Tier 2 - Dim_PlayerLevel.Name with LowBronze/HighBronze split at $1K equity)
merge.attribution: (Tier 1 - Dictionary.PlayerLevel; Tier 2 - Dim_PlayerLevel.Name with LowBronze/HighBronze split at $1K equity)
merge.notes: Upstream gives canonical tier list and Tier 1 source; downstream's LowBronze/HighBronze split is local logic that must be preserved

downstream_desc: 1 if customer was contacted through bonus/CRM channel on this date (Tier 2 - BI_DB_NewBonusReport.IsContacted)
upstream_desc: Contact status flag: 1 = account manager has contacted this customer about the event; 0 = not yet contacted. 97% of rows are 0 (uncontacted) in recent data. (Tier 3 — data evidence)

merge.notes: Upstream adds account-manager framing and distribution stat (97% zeros) but downstream already captures the business meaning; distribution stats are volatile and not worth merging

downstream_desc: Date when customer first satisfied the IsFunded_New criteria (first VL3-verified funded day). NULL if never funded under new definition (Tier 2 - BI_DB_CIDFirstDates.FirstNewFundedDate)
upstream_desc: **First date the customer crossed the fully-funded threshold.** Computed as GREATEST(FTDDateID, FirstVerifiedDateID, LEAST(FirstTradeDateID, FirstIOBDateID, FirstOptionsTradeDateID)) — the date when all four funded criteria were first simultaneously satisfied. Set once via WHERE FirstNewFundedDate IS NULL, never overwritten. Source: Function_Population_First_Time_Funded. (Tier 1 — Function_Population_First_Time_Funded)

merge.merged_desc: Date when customer first satisfied all funded criteria simultaneously. Computed as GREATEST(FTDDateID, FirstVerifiedDateID, LEAST(FirstTradeDateID, FirstIOBDateID, FirstOptionsTradeDateID)) via Function_Population_First_Time_Funded. Set once (WHERE FirstNewFundedDate IS NULL), never overwritten. NULL if never funded under new definition. (Tier 1 - Function_Population_First_Time_Funded; Tier 2 - BI_DB_CIDFirstDates.FirstNewFundedDate)
merge.notes: Upstream provides the exact GREATEST/LEAST formula, immutability guarantee, and Tier 1 source function

downstream_desc: Name of the acquisition funnel the customer entered, from `DWH_dbo.Dim_Funnel.Name` on `Dim_Customer.FunnelID`. NULL if no funnel. (Tier 2 — Dim_Funnel.Name via Dim_Customer.FunnelID)
upstream_desc: Unique human-readable label for the funnel. Used in marketing reports, BackOffice customer views, and attribution analytics. Describes the campaign/channel/product that drove registration. (Tier 1 — Dictionary.Funnel)

merge.merged_desc: Name of the acquisition funnel the customer entered. Human-readable label describing the campaign, channel, or product that drove registration; used in marketing reports, BackOffice customer views, and attribution analytics. Sourced from DWH_dbo.Dim_Funnel.Name via Dim_Customer.FunnelID. NULL if no funnel is assigned.
merge.attribution: (Tier 1 — Dim_Funnel.Name / Dictionary.Funnel; Tier 2 — joined via Dim_Customer.FunnelID)
merge.notes: Upstream adds business context: funnel label describes campaign/channel/product and is used in marketing and attribution reporting.

downstream_desc: Name of the source funnel (referral funnel), from `DWH_dbo.Dim_Funnel.Name` on `Dim_Customer.FunnelFromID`. LEFT JOIN — NULL when no source funnel. (Tier 2 — Dim_Funnel.Name via Dim_Customer.FunnelFromID)
upstream_desc: Unique human-readable label for the funnel. Used in marketing reports, BackOffice customer views, and attribution analytics. Describes the campaign/channel/product that drove registration. (Tier 1 — Dictionary.Funnel)

merge.merged_desc: Name of the source (referral) funnel. Human-readable label describing the campaign, channel, or product that drove the referring customer's registration; used in marketing reports, BackOffice customer views, and attribution analytics. Sourced from DWH_dbo.Dim_Funnel.Name via Dim_Customer.FunnelFromID. LEFT JOIN — NULL when no source funnel exists.
merge.attribution: (Tier 1 — Dim_Funnel.Name / Dictionary.Funnel; Tier 2 — joined via Dim_Customer.FunnelFromID)
merge.notes: Upstream adds business context: funnel label describes campaign/channel/product; downstream correctly notes referral semantics and LEFT JOIN NULL behavior, both preserved.
